danger_signals.visual_form: water terrain surrounding and below level
danger_signals.meaning: instant death zone; worms knocked into water die regardless of health

danger_signals.visual_form: weapon trajectory indicators and targeting reticles
danger_signals.meaning: imminent damage to worms in blast radius
proximity to death state; loss of hitpoints from weapon impact

danger_signals.visual_form: opposing worms on terrain marked by team color
danger_signals.meaning: active threat; worms capable of dealing damage on their turn

danger_signals.visual_form: crater formations and missing terrain tiles after explosions
danger_signals.meaning: environmental hazard; worms may fall into newly created gaps or water

safety_signals.meaning: stable position; worm can stand without falling
reduced immediate threat; enemies cannot reach in current turn

safety_signals.visual_form: airdropped weapon and utility crates with distinctive visual markers
safety_signals.meaning: resource availability; access to weapons and items for offensive/defensive action

safety_signals.visual_form: UI indicator showing which team is active
safety_signals.meaning: inactive teams cannot be targeted; safe from direct attack during enemy turns

faction_indicators.color_identity: player-selectable team colors (red, blue, green, yellow, etc.)
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: worm sprites rendered in team color
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: all teams use identical worm models; differentiation purely through color assignment
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: team color extends to health bars, UI elements, and weapon crate ownership indicators

faction_indicators.color_identity: matched team colors signal alliance membership
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: allied worms rendered in same color; appear as unified force visually despite separate control
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: shared weapon pool and score display indicate alliance relationship

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: full, intact terrain; all worms at starting positions; weapon crates distributed evenly
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: limited weapon availability; teams select opening strategies based on standard weapon sets

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: partially destructed terrain with craters; scattered crate remnants; multiple worms eliminated (fewer sprites visible)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: increased tactical positioning; players seek high-ground advantage; resource scarcity drives crate-hunting behavior

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: heavily fragmented terrain; few remaining worms; concentrated water hazards from terrain loss
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: conservative movement; high-risk weapon usage; focus shifts to survival positioning

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: UI notification and weapon availability timer; distinct weapon slot highlights team-specific super-weapons
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: aggressive plays when special weapons unlock; teams reset tactical priorities to deploy devastating attacks

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: turn order indicator cycles through team colors in UI
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: worm behavior changes from active (mobile, weapon-enabled) to passive (stationary, defensive stance)

environmental_storytelling.example: crater formations left behind after explosions serve as visual record of previous engagements; terrain shape tells narrative of combat intensity
removal of worm sprites from board communicates defeat; absence of color-coded worms indicates team progress toward victory
density and placement of airdropped crates shifts environmental narrative from abundant resources to scarcity-driven gameplay
map design with cliffs, islands, and water boundaries creates implicit narrative of environmental hazards as primary opponent
cartoonish explosion effects (sheep blast, skunk gas cloud) communicate tone and establish thematic consistency with humorous world

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: can walk, jump, use rope/parachute/teleport/bungee mechanics; actively controlled by player; equipped with selected weapon
static on terrain; cannot be moved or controlled; remains vulnerable to enemy attacks
visual feedback through health bar near depletion; may exhibit knockback or recoil animations from recent weapon hits
removal from board; sprite disappears; team worm count decreases; no further participation in match
autonomous movement toward player worms or strategic positions; selects weapons based on difficulty setting; exhibits increasing aggression in deathmatch mode
animated descent onto terrain with visual trajectory; communicates random reinforcement mechanic
static visual marker on terrain; accessible to any worm that touches it; team color association determines ownership

notes: Worms Armageddon communicates game state primarily through spatial visualization on a 2D destructible board. Team identity is purely color-coded with no faction-specific symbols or sigils—differentiation is cosmetic and mechanical rather than narrative. Danger is communicated through persistent environmental hazards (water), turn-based vulnerability states, and health bar reduction. The cartoonish art style and humorous weapon designs (sheep as mobile bomb, skunk gas) establish a comedic tone that supersedes serious world-state narrative. Faction relationships are determined by player-selected team color matching for alliances; this is a mechanical construct without lore basis. World-state changes are telegraphed through terrain destruction persistence, worm elimination, and weapon availability timers. The game lacks environmental storytelling in the narrative sense but uses destruction patterns and spatial configuration as implicit communication of combat history.
